no-stubs.title: Stub / placeholder / not-implemented marker
no-stubs.pattern: (?:for now|in a real app|placeholder\s+(?:for now|implementation|impl|until)\b|TODO: ?implement|not implemented)
no-stubs.description: Stub or deferred-work marker — global rule forbids stubs/placeholders/workarounds.
no-stubs.resolution: Implement the real behavior now; remove the placeholder.

ts-suppress.title: TypeScript suppression directive
ts-suppress.description: Suppressing the type checker instead of fixing the cause.
ts-suppress.resolution: Fix the underlying type error; remove the suppression.

as-any.title: `as any` cast
as-any.description: Escape-hatch cast that disables type safety.
as-any.resolution: Use a precise type or a discriminated narrowing.

as-any.title: `: any` type annotation
as-any.description: Explicit `any` annotation disables type checking at the boundary.
as-any.resolution: Use a precise type, unknown plus narrowing, or a generic constraint.

as-any.title: `Array<any>` generic
as-any.resolution: Use a precise element type or Array<unknown> with narrowing at reads.

as-any.title: `Promise<any>` generic
as-any.description: `Promise<any>` hides the async value contract from callers.
as-any.resolution: Return a precise promise type or Promise<unknown> with explicit narrowing.
as-any.canary: async function load(): Promise<any> { return fetchJson(); }
negativeCanary: async function load(): Promise<unknown> { return fetchJson(); }

as-any.title: `Record<string, any>` generic
as-any.description: `Record<string, any>` erases the value contract for dictionary-shaped data.
as-any.resolution: Use a precise value type, Record<string, unknown>, or a validated schema type.

no-narration-comments.title: Narrated / changelog-style comment
no-narration-comments.description: Narration/changelog comments that describe code history, removals, or reviewer-facing notes instead of explaining a non-obvious invariant or constraint.
no-narration-comments.resolution: Delete the narration. If context is still needed, keep a short comment about the non-obvious constraint or move history/cleanup notes to the commit message or issue.
negativeCanary: // removed duplicates before sending to the API
// changed to avoid recomputation on every keystroke
// removed trailing whitespace to satisfy the linter
// workaround for upstream bug in libfoo v2, see issue #123
// memoize because this is called in a hot loop
// guards against a null race between init and teardown

markers.title: TODO / FIXME / HACK marker comment
markers.description: Marker comments like TODO/FIXME/HACK leave known debt in shipped source.
markers.resolution: Do the work now or replace the marker with a concrete issue reference outside the source file.
negativeCanary: const msg = "# FIXME this is fixture text";
const block = "/* HACK left in sample text */";

test-integrity.title: Disabled or focused test runner call
test-integrity.description: Disabled or focused test calls remove coverage or narrow execution and should not ship.
test-integrity.resolution: Re-enable the test and remove focused execution before committing.

raw-hex.title: Hardcoded hex color
raw-hex.description: Raw hex color in source instead of a design token.
raw-hex.resolution: Use a CSS custom property / design token.

raw-hex.title: Magic px spacing instead of a design token
raw-hex.description: Hardcoded multi-digit px value in a style declaration — bypasses the spacing scale (ANTI-SLOP UX §15). Matches CSS (margin: 24px) and JS object/inline styles (style={{ padding: '24px' }}).
raw-hex.resolution: Use a spacing token / utility scale (var(--space-md), Tailwind p-4) instead of a raw px literal.

raw-hex.title: Hardcoded rgb()/hsl() color
raw-hex.description: Raw rgb()/hsl() color literal instead of a design token (ANTI-SLOP UX §15).
raw-hex.resolution: Use a CSS custom property / token; a token may itself wrap rgb(var(--…)).

kv-ban.title: Cloudflare KV usage
kv-ban.description: KV is eventually-consistent; banned for stateful/read-after-write paths.
kv-ban.resolution: Use a Durable Object (strong consistency) or cache.default (read caching).

live-secrets.title: Live secret literal in source
live-secrets.description: Stripe/webhook/Google API live credential hardcoded in source.
live-secrets.resolution: Move to a secret manager or environment variable; never commit live keys.

eval-ban.title: Dynamic code evaluation (eval / new Function)
eval-ban.description: Dynamic code evaluation creates XSS/injection surface.
eval-ban.resolution: Eliminate eval(); use static dispatch tables or data-driven config.

pii-logs.title: PII field in console.log
pii-logs.description: PII fields logged — may appear in log tails and error trackers.
pii-logs.resolution: Remove log or redact sensitive fields before logging.

weak-hash.title: Weak hash algorithm (MD5/SHA-1)
weak-hash.description: MD5/SHA-1 are cryptographically broken — forbidden for integrity checks and unusable for passwords.
weak-hash.resolution: Use SHA-256+ for integrity; bcrypt/argon2/scrypt for passwords.

env-safety.title: Fallback on process.env.HOME
env-safety.description: Fallbacking when HOME is missing masks a broken environment and can redirect state into the wrong path.
env-safety.resolution: Require HOME to be present and fail loudly if it is missing.

sql-safety.title: FOR UPDATE on aggregate query (SQL runtime error)
sql-safety.description: SQL FOR UPDATE with an aggregate function (SUM/COUNT/etc.) — Postgres (and SQL standard) rejects this at runtime: "FOR UPDATE is not allowed with aggregate functions".
sql-safety.resolution: Remove FOR UPDATE from aggregate queries. Only use FOR UPDATE on plain row-locking SELECTs: SELECT ... FROM t WHERE id = $1 FOR UPDATE.
sql-safety.canary: const q = `SELECT SUM(amount) FROM ledger WHERE user_id = $1 FOR UPDATE`;
negativeCanary: const q = `SELECT id FROM users WHERE id = $1 FOR UPDATE`;
const q = `SELECT SUM(amount) FROM ledger GROUP by user_id`;
